I oversee the signaling design for two metro lines.
I've worked in railway signaling for eight years.
I've been working with German suppliers since 2019.
I spent two months in San Francisco in 2023.
Good morning. I'm Anna Weber, project manager on the signaling package. Before we open the schedule, tell me a bit about your role at Motiva.
Of course. I'm a signaling engineer at Motiva, and I oversee the commissioning of the signaling system on two metro lines.
And how long have you been in signaling?
I've worked in railway signaling for eight years, and I've been working with European suppliers since 2019. In 2023, I spent two months in San Francisco on a field commissioning job.
Good. Then you know the pain. Our next milestone is the factory inspection in Berlin, and the test report is the first deliverable. What worries you most?
Honestly, the deadline. We've been waiting for the updated specification since March, and compliance with the European standard is mandatory for us.
I'm a signaling engineer at Motiva, and I oversee the commissioning.
My main concern is the deadline for the factory inspection.
Sorry, could you rephrase that? I want to make sure I understand.

Hello João, this is Anna Weber from the signaling package. I am calling to confirm the date of the factory inspection in Berlin. We have finally sent the updated specification, so your team can start the review this week. The inspection is now planned for the second week of October, and the test report will follow within ten working days. Please bring the compliance checklist from your team, because we need to close the open points before we sign the acceptance certificate. If the date does not work for you, let me know by Friday and I will speak to our production manager. Thank you, and I look forward to seeing you in Berlin.

Good morning, everyone, and thank you for joining the monthly status meeting. I want to start with the schedule. The first deliverable, the test report, is now three weeks late, and that delay puts pressure on our next milestone. The good news is that the specification has finally arrived and the design review is complete. I also want to be very clear about one point. The extra training sessions the client asked for are outside the scope of the contract, and we will treat them as a change request, not as a free service. Before the next inspection, I want a realistic deadline that every stakeholder can accept, not an optimistic one that we miss again. Compliance with the European standard is not negotiable, so let us build the plan around it.
